coredata.dc:title: Synergistic photocatalytic dye mitigation and bacterial disinfection using carbon quantum dots decorated dual Z-scheme Manganese Indium Sulfide/Cuprous Oxide/Silver oxide heterojunction
coredata.dc:creator: Sharma, Sheetal
Dutta, Vishal
Raizada, Pankaj
Kumar Thakur, Vijay
Saini, Adesh K.
Mittal, Divya
Nguyen, Van-Huy
Ahamad, Tansir
Chien Nguyen, Chinh
Young Kim, Soo
Le, Quyet Van
Singh, Pardeep
coredata.dc:description: A novel CQDs decorated MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O dual Z-scheme tandem hybrid is fabricated via hydrothermal method for MO removal and E. coli disinfection under visible light illumination. CQDs shuttle electrons in CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O interface via dual Z-scheme electron transferal route which lead to upgrade in charge separation and visible light absorption. The Z-scheme hybrid displayed best catalytic inactivation performance of E. coli and degradation of MO which totally disinfect bacteria within 3 h and displayed 98.6% of MO degradation in 70 min in visible light radiation. The unique dual Z-scheme junction supports enhanced redox capabilities, prolong lifespan of photo-excitons, stability, spatial separation and enhanced visible light absorption.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Dual Z-scheme
Tandem charge transfer
MO removal
E. coli disinfection

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) SEM image, (b) EDX spectra, (c-d) TEM and HRTEM image, (e-h) XRD patterns of fabricated sample.

(a) UV and (b-f) Tauc plots of fabricated samples. (g) PL of (i) Cu2O, (ii) Ag2O, (iii) MnIn2S4, (iv) CQDs, (v) CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O. (h) FT-IR spectra of CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O, (ii) Cu2O, (iii) Ag2O (iv) MnIn2S4. (i) EIS curve of (i) blank (ii) MnIn2S4, (iii) CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O (iv) MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O.

(a) MO mineralization under visible light over various photocatalysts (b) Recycling experiments (c) HPLC and (d) GC–MS spectra of MO (e) Trapping studies of ROS over heterojunction (f) Antibacterial study of CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O (g) Photocatalytic mechanism for MO removal and (h) bacterial disinfection.

head.ce:title: Synergistic photocatalytic dye mitigation and bacterial disinfection using carbon quantum dots decorated dual Z-scheme Manganese Indium Sulfide/Cuprous Oxide/Silver oxide heterojunction

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A novel CQDs decorated MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O dual Z-scheme tandem hybrid is fabricated via hydrothermal method for MO removal and E. coli disinfection under visible light illumination. CQDs shuttle electrons in CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O interface via dual Z-scheme electron transferal route which lead to upgrade in charge separation and visible light absorption. The Z-scheme hybrid displayed best catalytic inactivation performance of E. coli and degradation of MO which totally disinfect bacteria within 3 h and displayed 98.6% of MO degradation in 70 min in visible light radiation. The unique dual Z-scheme junction supports enhanced redox capabilities, prolong lifespan of photo-excitons, stability, spatial separation and enhanced visible light absorption.

ce:section.ce:para: With substantial increase in water pollution, elimination of pollutants from the water bodies has attained extensive concern. As an outcome, employing heterogeneous photocatalysis to decontaminate or disinfect wastewater has acquired much significance [1–3]. Still, single-phase photocatalysts encounter limitations on their catalytic activity, holding restricted spectra activity, low quantum yield and higher recombination of charge carrier. Likewise, it is principally difficult in a single catalytic system to comprise both strong oxidation capacities of holes and reduction capacities of electrons [4,5]. These limitations have been efficiently organized through heterojunction construction employing different photocatalysts with apt bandgap for supreme optical absorption [6].

Manganese Indium sulfide (MnIn2S4; band gap of ∼ 2.0 eV) exhibits excellent catalytic activity and great photo-stability under visible light illumination. Cuprous oxide (Cu2O) having band gap = 1.9 eV which has acquired attention due to abundance in nature, low toxicity and high visible light absorption. However, application of Cu2O as a photocatalysts is hampered by quick recombination of the electron-hole pair and agglomeration, thus incorporation of other appropriate photocatalysts can improve its photostability.Silver oxide (Ag2O), p-type semiconductor photocatalyst possessing band gap = 1.2 eV. It has been acknowledged as one of promising substitutes to conventional photocatalysts due to its superior absorption capacity of visible light. Carbon quantum dots (CQDs), are considered to be exclusive contenders of interfacial alteration because of electron transfer ability, good biocompatibility and used in catalysis as an vibrant part of Z-scheme along with dual Z-scheme based hybrids [1,4].

Promoted by eco-friendly technique, constructing novel dual Z-scheme CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O to mineralize MO and E.coli inactivation is objective of present research. XRD, TEM, SEM, UV, EDX, FT-IR, EIS techniques were employed to characterize various properties of samples. To conclude, mechanistic pathway to enhanced catalysis has been grounded on double tandem Z-scheme charge transferal.
The fabrications and photocatalytic experimentations are depicted in supplementary document.

SEM images of heterojunction represents flat cabbage leaf like morphology and CQDs deposition amid different leaf like structures was also observed (Fig. 1 a). The fibrous surface of heterojunction offered low density, excellent structural performance and larger surface area for different catalytic reactions. The average diameter of heterojunction nanoparticles were estimated by plotting histogram and gaussian distribution function and estimated average diameter was 100 nm. Fig. 1b display EDX spectrum of heterojunction. Fig. 1c,d displays TEM and HRTEM mage of heterojunction and lattice fringes with spacing of 0.332 nm correspond to (104) rhombohedral planes of MnIn2S4. The d-spacing of 0.25 nm agree with (006) rhombohedral lattice of Ag2O and interplanar spacing of 0.245 nm correspond to (110) hexagonal planes of Cu2O. HRTEM images reveals, d-spacing of 0.22 nm attribute to (002) planes of CQDs. XRD studies were done to determine crystal structure of the heterojunction (Fig. 1e). The lattice planes (111), (104) and (211) corresponded to 14.3°, 39.8° and 53.8°, respectively shows peaks for rhombohedral MnIn2S4 with space group R-3C (JCPDS no. 65–7474). The peak for cubic Cu2O seen at (104) corresponded to 74.2° with space group Pn3m (JCPDS no. 05–0667). The peaks for cubic Ag2O observed at (104), (006), (113), (211) and (211) planes indexed to 24.2°, 33.4°, 33.9° and 51.2°, respectively (JCPDS no. 72–1214). The peaks for CQDs seen at (104) and (002) lattice planes indexed to 29.3° and 42.8°, respectively. Surface area of heterojunction were examined by N2 adsorption–desorption isotherm at 77 K (Fig. S1).

XPS spectra of N 1s, C 1s, Mn 2p, In 3d, O 1s, Ag 3d, Cu 2p and S 2p of CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O heterojunction is exhibited Fig. 2 a-h. The survey XPS is presented in Fig. 2i. The peaks for Mn are observed at 643.4 and 651.1 eV accredited to Mn 2p3/2 and 2p1/2. For In, peaks seen at 444.2 and 452 eV indexed to In 3d5/2 and 3d3/2, respectively. The peak centered at 284.31 eV is attributed to carbon, perceived in the carbon nitrides spectrum. N 1s spectrum explains peaks at 397.2 and 398.5 eV corresponds to C-N–H and N–H, respectively. The O 1s spectra elaborates binding energies at 528.2 and 530.6 eV corresponds to C–N–H and C=O, respectively. Spectra for S 2p represents peaks at 157.2 eV and 162.23 eV indexed to S 2p3/2 and 2p1/2, respectively. XPS spectra for Ag showed peaks 367.8 and 371.3 eV ascribed to Ag 3d5/2 and Ag 3d3/2, respectively. For Cu, peaks spotted at 934.9 and 941.8 eV indexed to Cu 2p3/2 and Cu 2p1/2 respectively.

In UV–vis spectrum (Fig. 3 a), enhanced absorption intensity of hybrid could be witnessed due to the CQDs, where they performed as photosensitizer and photocenter for the absorption of light. The band gaps associated with Cu2O, CQDs, Ag2O, MnIn2S4 and CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O were calculated by plotting Tauc’s plot (Fig. 3b-f). To study separation frequency of produced electron- hole pairs, the PL experiments were carried out (Fig. 3g). Fig. 3h depicts functional groups and chemical bonds of prepared catalysts via FTIR. Further, EIS Nyquist plots were plotted as shown in Fig. 3i. Results demonstrated that CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O exhibit smallest arc radius which indicates highest electronic conductivity on heterojunction interface and improved separation of electron-hole pair.

MO removal under visible light radiation over various photocatalysts, as depicted in Fig. 4 a. CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O displayed 98.6% MO degradation in 70 min. To experiment reusability of CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O, photodegradation experiments were recurred for ten cycles (Fig. 4b) and showed high photo-stability of heterojunction throughout recycling experimentations. The photodegradation and formation of intermediates were studied by HPLC and GC–MS (Fig. 4c,d). The trapping experimentation was further performed to explore ROS that exhibit a major role in MO removal (Fig. 4e). Besides, catalytic performance of hybrid was confirmed. Fig. 4f shows decline in quantity of viable colonies of E. coli with time and results revealed that hybrid possess high disinfection efficacy against E. coli. A plausible dual tandem Z-scheme mechanism for MO mineralization in visible light was projected as displayed in Fig. 4g. The VB and CB potentials of MnIn2S4 are −0.74 and 1.15 eV, respectively. CB and VB positions of Cu2O and Ag2O are −1.3 and −0.2 and 0.8 eV and 1.3 eV, respectively. CB electrons of MnIn2S4 and VB holes of Cu2O transferred to CQDs and combined rapidly. Similarly, CB electrons of Ag2O and VB holes of Cu2O transferred to the CQDs and combined. This route of charge transferal is known as double Z-scheme tandem charge transferal. Since CB potential of Cu2O (-1.3 vs NHE) and MnIn2S4 (-0.74 vs NHE) is negative than
E
0
(
O 2
/
∙ O
2
-
= -0.33 eV), the electrons on Cu2O CB and MnIn2S4 yield O2 into
∙
O2 −, which further resulted in removal of MO dye. Hence, construction of dual Z-scheme CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O junction promoted charge separation ability, enhanced redox ability and expands lifetime of excitons. Similarly, possible mechanism for antibacterial mechanism is sketched in Fig. 4h. The reactive oxidative species (ROS) invade bacteria cell wall, cause degeneration and outflow of cytoplasm, leading to bacteria death. The life time of electron-hole pairs in CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O is prolonged due to conjugation of photo-generated electrons and deposition of CQDs on heterojunction, and thus enhance bacterial and photocatalytic performances.

Herein, dual tandem Z-scheme CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O was fabricated successfully using hydrothermal method. CQDs acted as receptor and donor which promoted photo-generated charge transferal.
∙
O2 −, e− and h+ as effective reactive species accredited to MO mineralization and E. coli disinfection. In framework, novel and effective dual tandem Z-scheme CQDs-MnIn2S4/Cu2O/Ag2O heterojunction will have extensive application outlook in wastewater treatment in future.

Sheetal Sharma: Conceptualization, Methodology, Software, Data curation, Formal analysis, Writing – original draft. Vishal Dutta: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Pankaj Raizada: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Vijay Kumar Thakur: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Adesh K. Saini: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Divya Mittal: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Van-Huy Nguyen: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Tansir Ahamad: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Chinh Chien Nguyen: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Soo Young Kim: Visualization, Resources, Formal analysis, Investigation. Quyet Van Le: Supervision, Validation, Resources, Writing – review & editing. Pardeep Singh: Supervision, Validation, Resources, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was supported by Brain Pool Program through the National Research Foundation of Korea (NRF) funded by the Ministry of Science and ICT (Grant Number: 2020H1D3A1A04081409). The authors thank the Researchers Supporting Project number (RSP-2021/6), King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
